entries.title: Test suite red blocks structural review
Test helper re-exports stdlib it does not use, generating pervasive lint noise
make_args() has no contract-sync test — will drift again on next arg addition
PTH108 + I001 lint violations in run_review.py (pre-existing)
run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure

finding_snapshot.title: run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: run_review() is a large coordinating function; the two-attempt loop + codex-setup phases remain inline.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: The remaining phases thread use_resume/session_id/fallback_used/returncode in+out, so extracting them adds ceremony.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: A 9-anchor structure would have every cohesive phase behind a named helper; the residual flat region holds simplicity below the anchor.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Accept the inline phases as irreducible, OR pursue the marginal CodexCapture value-return.

entries.title: make_args() dict still manually maintained — two-step edit requirement persists

finding_snapshot.title: make_args() dict still manually maintained — two-step edit requirement persists
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: make_args() restated the run_review.py CLI arg contract and defaults in a hand-maintained 17-key dict; the next arg addition required a two-step edit and caused the loop-1 build failure.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:make_args() carried a parallel dict of arg names + defaults instead of deriving them from parse_args().
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: Duplicated authority over the arg-name set is a drift hazard that already produced a real build failure.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Derive make_args() defaults from run_review.parse_args() under a saved/restored sys.argv; delete the dict and the now-unused argparse import.

entries.title: README documents a test command that collects zero tests + a stale test count

finding_snapshot.title: README documents a test command that collects zero tests + a stale test count
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: A new contributor (or an AI agent) following README.md to run the suite is told to run 'cd scripts && python3 -m pytest test_run_review.py test_web_search.py' (README.md:52) against files described as the pytest suite (README.md:41-42). One of those files does not exist and the other is a unittest discovery shim, so the documented command collects zero tests — the reader would conclude the project is untested. This undermines the credibility of an otherwise well-tested adapter (119 real tests live under scripts/tests/).
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: README.md:42 references scripts/test_web_search.py which does not exist (web-search tests were not split into a pytest module). README.md:41 claims 'test_run_review.py pytest suite (118 tests)' — stale count (the suite is 119) and mislabeled (test_run_review.py is a 16-LoC unittest.TestLoader().discover shim, not a pytest test module). The canonical command is 'python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/'.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: A documented entrypoint that silently collects zero tests is a doc-vs-code credibility leak per method.md Step 6: a reviewer trusting the README would believe the suite is empty or broken. It is the kind of small honesty gap the rubric penalizes when it would mislead a reader about regression resistance.
evidence: README.md:41 'test_run_review.py  pytest suite (118 tests)' — stale count (actual 119) + mislabel (shim, not pytest module)
README.md:42 'test_web_search.py  web-search adapter pytest suite' — file does not exist (ls scripts/test_web_search.py -> No such file)

README.md:52 'cd scripts && python3 -m pytest test_run_review.py test_web_search.py' — verified to print 'no tests ran' (zero collected) because test_web_search.py is missing and test_run_review.py exposes no pytest-discoverable test functions
scripts/test_run_review.py is a unittest.TestLoader().discover(str(here / 'tests'), ...) shim (16 LoC), not a pytest test module
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Update README.md to document the canonical command 'python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/'; correct the test count to 119; drop or correct the test_web_search.py reference (point at the actual web-search tests or remove the stale line). Subtractive where possible (delete the dead reference).
